mission.title: kserve: Issues with KFServing as part of a Kubeflow install
mission.description: Issues with KFServing as part of a Kubeflow install. This issue affects 10+ users.

steps.title: Identify kserve troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kserve deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve -n kserve --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kserve configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kserve configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve
kubectl get configmap -n kserve -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kserve
```
I'm seeing some issues with the KFServing install that's part of the 'out-of-the-box' Kubeflow install (0.7.1).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Issues with KFServing as part of a Kubeflow install
steps.description:
In kubeflow installation we have two istio gateway installed, they both serve on http port 80 and route to the same istio ingress gateway, so one way to solve the issue is to configure knative to use the kubeflow gateway instead.
```
kubectl get gateways --all-namespaces
NAMESPACE         NAME     
```yaml
Error from server: error when creating "kfserving-tf-flowers.yaml": admission webhook "inferenceservice.kfserving-webhook-server.validator" denied the request: Cannot create the Inferenceservice "flowers-sample" in namespace "kubeflow": the namespace lacks label "serving.kubeflow.org/inferenceservice: enabled"
```

steps.title: Confirm Issues with KFServing as part of a Kubeflow… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kserve -n kserve --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kserve --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: In kubeflow installation we have two istio gateway installed, they both serve on http port 80 and route to the same istio ingress gateway, so one way to solve the issue is to configure knative to use the kubeflow gateway instead.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kserve installed or the issue environment reproducible.
